greeting: A leader fights for everyone under his command.
blunder: If I made decisions like that, my squad would've promoted a bucket to command.
Your pieces aren't working together. They're filing formal complaints against each other.
That move had the confidence of a genius and the results of a kitchen accident.
check: Check. Your army seems just as surprised as your king.
capture: And there goes another soldier under your command.
promotion: A promotion. At least someone in your ranks is doing their job.
default: My promotion came after a difficult campaign.
I remember the soldiers who never returned.
Leadership sounded glorious when I was young.
Every mistake becomes someone else's burden.
victory: Skill alone isn't enough. Learn responsibility before seeking higher ranks.
I've seen headless chickens coordinate an attack better than that.
defeat: You've proven you can lead your own path.
